notes: Test job ran `just gen-all` on a depot-ubuntu runner. The Justfile recipe `gen-all` calls `format` (line 32) which calls `pnpm format` (line 221), but `pnpm` is not installed in the runner image. `sh: 1: pnpm: not found` → exit 127 propagates through the recipe stack to the GHA `##[error]Process completed with exit code 127.`. Workflow setup error (CI didn't install pnpm before running gen-all). Source URL: https://github.com/biomejs/biome/actions/runs/25469302190/job/74729494217. Imported on 2026-05-06 (per session date 2026-05-07); raw.log normalized from `rtk proxy gh run view --job <id> --log` output.
root_cause_summary: The biome `Test` job's `just gen-all` step failed because `pnpm` is not installed in the runner image. The Justfile recipe stack is: `gen-all` (line 32) → `format` (line 221) → `pnpm format`. The shell rejected `pnpm format` with `sh: 1: pnpm: not found` (exit 127), and `just` propagated the failure up the recipe chain (`error: Recipe format failed on line 221 with exit code 127`, then `error: Recipe gen-all failed on line 32 with exit code 127`), which surfaces in GHA as `##[error]Process completed with exit code 127.`. This is a workflow-setup error: the workflow needs to install pnpm (e.g. via a `setup-pnpm` action) before running `just gen-all`.

notes: apache/dubbo PR integration tests (~37min). Test `dubbo-samples-test-11137` timed out — `[2/23] [dubbo-samples-test-11137:1/1] TEST FAILURE: Run tests timeout, version: -Ddubbo.version=3.3.7-SNAPSHOT -Dspring.version=5.3.24 -Djava.version=21` at L4891 of 6095 (~80%, late). The test orchestrates docker-compose containers (nacos, mysql, dubbo-samples-test-11137-test); when the test framework hits its timeout, it aggressively tears down containers — the post-failure log shows `Error response from daemon: No such container` for each (the containers were already gone by the time the cleanup scripts ran). Selected as Batch 6 case 1 — fills java-maven ecosystem gap (Apache Dubbo uses Maven via dubbo-maven-plugin) and adds a docker-compose-orchestrated timeout pattern. Source: https://github.com/apache/dubbo/actions/runs/25539178788/job/74961870539. Privacy audit: 0 hits, complete_scan=True. failure_category=timeout_or_oom; framework=generic (Dubbo's integration-test runner orchestrates docker-compose, not pytest/jest).
root_cause_summary: apache/dubbo PR integration test `dubbo-samples-test-11137` (test 2 of 23) timed out under the shared integration-test orchestrator on ubuntu-22.04 with Java 21. The orchestrator runs each sample via docker-compose with sidecar containers (nacos config server, nacos-mysql backing store, the dubbo-samples-test-11137 test container). When the per-test timeout hit, the framework aggressively tore down all three containers, then attempted to print logs from each — but the containers no longer existed, so the log shows repeated `Error response from daemon: No such container: dubbo-samples-test-11137--<container>-1` immediately preceding the `TEST FAILURE: Run tests timeout` summary line. The error message at L4891 names the test, the timeout cause, the dubbo/spring/java version under test, and points the developer at /home/runner/work/dubbo/dubbo/99-integration/dubbo-samples-test-11137/target/logs for per-container detail. The summary repeats at L6000 (after a few more samples ran), then `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1` at L6010 propagates to GHA exit 1.

notes: Single Go test failure on macos-latest only: TestHuhPrompterMultiSelectWithSearchPersistence/selections_persist_after_changing_search_query in github.com/cli/cli/v2/internal/prompter, with message `form.Run() did not complete in time` at huh_prompter_test.go:478. flaky_or_transient = true (macOS timing flake; Linux build presumably passed). signal_position = middle (FAIL block at L243-247 of 545 lines ≈ 45%) — primary diagnostic OUTSIDE tail-200's window (L346-545), so tail-200 sees only bare `FAIL` + `##[error]` at L510-511 without the failing test name or message. Source URL: https://github.com/cli/cli/actions/runs/25420916282/job/74562623100. Imported on 2026-05-06; raw.log normalized from `rtk proxy gh run view --job <id> --log` output by stripping the leading <job>\t<step>\t prefix per line.
root_cause_summary: The Go test `TestHuhPrompterMultiSelectWithSearchPersistence/selections_persist_after_changing_search_query` in package `github.com/cli/cli/v2/internal/prompter` (file `huh_prompter_test.go:478`) failed on the macos-latest runner with the message `form.Run() did not complete in time`. The test wraps a TUI prompter form; the failure indicates `form.Run()` did not return before the test's internal time bound elapsed — characteristic of a timing flake on the slower macOS runner rather than a logic bug. Only this single subtest in only this single OS configuration failed, driving the macos-latest job to exit code 1.

notes: go-redis Go test against redis-ce 8.4.x oldstable (~8min). Ginkgo test `PubSub [It] should ChannelMessage` (pubsub_test.go:645) failed: `[FAILED] Timed out after 1.001s. Expected <<-chan *redis.Message | len:0, cap:10>: 0xc0012d96c0 to receive something.` (L1106-1109). Pattern is a per-test timeout: the test waits for a redis-ce server to publish a Pub/Sub message and the channel never received one in 1.001s. Macroscopically: 1223 Passed | 1 Failed | 0 Pending | 50 Skipped (L1171). Late signal_position (failure block at L1102-1172 of 2730, ~42% — but wait, this is the FIRST failure in the log; everything after L1172 is more passing tests then the FAIL summary at L1331-1333. So actually middle/scattered? The TestGinkgoSuite block failure surfaces TWICE — first detail at L1102 then summary at L1166-1172, then the package-level FAIL marker much later at L1331. Calling this signal_position=middle since the actual diagnostic content is centered around 40% of the log). Selected as Batch 5 case 3 — hold-out, NOT in calibration set. Privacy audit: 0 hits after redacting test fixture URLs (TestParseURL subtests with placeholder credentials) and truncating 2 ginkgo bullet-decoration lines. failure_category=timeout_or_oom (per-test timeout, same shape as the nodejs case in calibration set — but on a different ecosystem and from a different source, so it's a hold-out generalization test of the timeout_or_oom category). framework=generic (Go's testing framework + ginkgo, not pytest/jest).
root_cause_summary: go-redis ginkgo test `PubSub [It] should ChannelMessage` failed in pubsub_test.go: the test subscribed to a Redis Pub/Sub channel and waited for a message via the read end of a buffered Go channel (`<-chan *redis.Message | len:0, cap:10`), but no message arrived within 1.001s and the test's gomega `Eventually(...).Should(Receive())` matcher timed out at pubsub_test.go:661. Ginkgo's failure block (L1102-1110) names the test, the test source line where the timeout fires (L661), and the failed channel state. Test runner summary at L1166-1172: `Summarizing 1 Failure: [FAIL] PubSub [It] should ChannelMessage pubsub_test.go:661 Ran 1224 of 1274 Specs in 179.248 seconds FAIL! -- 1223 Passed | 1 Failed | 0 Pending | 50 Skipped`. Likely cause: the redis-ce 8.4.x oldstable test server didn't deliver the published message within the 1s timeout — could be slow network setup or a transient redis-server flake under CI load. Package-level FAIL marker at L1331-1333 propagates to GHA exit code 2.

notes: Gradle's own Contributor CI build (~13min). Gradle task `:platform-base:projectHealth` failed because `platforms/software/platform-base/build.gradle.kts` declares `domainObjectCollections` as `api` when it should be `implementation`. The What-went-wrong block (L12772-12783) names the file path at L12776 and the suggested fix at L12778 (`implementation(projects.domainObjectCollections) (was api)`). projectHealth is Gradle's own internal lint plugin that enforces best-practice dependency scopes — `implementation` is preferred over `api` when downstream modules don't need transitive access. Late signal_position (~99%). Selected as Batch 5 case 2 — fills the v2 java-gradle ecosystem gap (kotlin DSL build script, on Gradle's own self-build). Source: https://github.com/gradle/gradle/actions/runs/25520527023/job/74903876543. Privacy audit: 0 hits, complete_scan=True. failure_category=lint_error (projectHealth is a code-quality lint check); framework=generic (Gradle's own contributor build matrix runner).
root_cause_summary: Gradle's Contributor CI build's `:platform-base:projectHealth` task failed because the build script `platforms/software/platform-base/build.gradle.kts` declares an `api` dependency on `projects.domainObjectCollections` that should be `implementation`. The projectHealth plugin (Gradle's internal best-practice lint) detected the unnecessary api-scope leak and reported it as `Existing dependencies which should be modified to be as indicated: implementation(projects.domainObjectCollections) (was api)` (L12776-12778). One task fails out of 6342 actionable tasks. Failure propagates: BUILD FAILED in 7m 41s (L12785) → ##[error]Process completed with exit code 1 (L12792).

notes: Hibernate ORM CI on OpenJDK 25 / hsqldb backend (~81min). Single test failure: `DbVersionTest > testCollectionVersion(SessionFactoryScope) FAILED` at L45536 of 46198 (~99%, late) with `org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>` at DbVersionTest.java:60. The test asserts that updating an entity's collection should NOT increment the owner entity's @Version field, but the actual behavior incremented it on hsqldb (other backends pass). This is a backend-specific assertion failure indicating a version-bump regression on hsqldb. Selected as Batch 6 case 2 — adds a third java-gradle case (third Hibernate-ecosystem stack: ORM-on-Gradle, complementing the existing spring-boot Gradle case from Batch 5). Source: https://github.com/hibernate/hibernate-orm/actions/runs/25540559564/job/74965317180. Privacy audit: 0 hits, complete_scan=True. log_size_bucket=large (46198 lines / 4.4 MB) — close to but not over 50000 huge threshold. failure_category=test_assertion; framework=generic (Gradle Test Executor 25 ran the JUnit 5 tests via Hibernate's custom Gradle plugin).
root_cause_summary: Hibernate ORM's CI run on OpenJDK 25 with hsqldb backend had a single test failure: `DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion` at hibernate-core/src/test/java/.../version/db/DbVersionTest.java:60. The test asserts (`assertFalse`) that the owner entity's @Version field SHOULD NOT be incremented when its child collection is mutated (because the test verifies a behavior where updating a collection's contents alone shouldn't bump the parent version) — but on hsqldb the actual behavior bumped it: `owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>`. The error trace shows the standard JUnit Jupiter assertion-failure stack (AssertionFailureBuilder.build → AssertFalse.failNotFalse → DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion). One test failed out of thousands; Gradle test runner reports `> Task :hibernate-core:test FAILED` (L45955) → `FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.` (L45958) → `BUILD FAILED in 17m 39s` (L46120) → `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1` (L46121). Other backend variants (mysql, mariadb, etc.) ran in parallel matrix legs and have their own failures, but this case is scoped to the OpenJDK 25 hsqldb leg only.

notes: C++ template-instantiation compile error in pandas's perf branch (`perf/skew-kurt-omp-xsimd`). The author added `extern template ... accumulate_moments_simd::operator()<xsimd::neon64>(...)` at moments_simd.hpp:255 (and the matching definition at moments_inst_neon.cpp:14), but the primary template was declared with `xsimd::neon` (not `xsimd::neon64`) at moments_simd.hpp:182. clang++ rejects the explicit instantiation. 2 compile units fail with the same root cause. macOS-15 / arm64 / Python 3.12 / clang++ -std=c++20. Source URL: https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/actions/runs/25463397447/job/74710897960. Imported on 2026-05-06; raw.log normalized from `rtk proxy gh run view --job <id> --log` output. Note: split=v2/holdout puts holdout at 5/4 (slightly over-target); v2/stress remains empty for cases truly fitting that split's 'deliberately difficult' criteria.
root_cause_summary: The pandas `Unit Tests / macos-15 py312` job's C++ extension build (meson+ninja under `pip install -e`) fails because `pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp:255` declares an explicit template instantiation `extern template Moments accumulate_moments_simd::operator()<xsimd::neon64>(...)` (and the matching non-extern definition at `pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp:14`), but the primary template at `moments_simd.hpp:182` is parameterized with `xsimd::neon`, not `xsimd::neon64`. clang++ -std=c++20 rejects with `error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member`, and the diagnostic note shows the type-mismatch directly: `could not match 'Moments (xsimd::neon64, ...)' against 'Moments (xsimd::neon, ...)'`. Two compile units fail with the same root cause (moments_inst_neon.cpp.o and moments.cpp.o); ninja stops the build, pip's metadata-generation hook exits 1.

notes: `pnpm audit` reported one moderate-severity vulnerability in transitive dep `ip-address` (XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods, GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g). Vulnerable versions: <=10.1.0; patched: >=10.1.1. The dependency path is network__fetch > @pnpm/network.agent > @pnpm/network.proxy-agent > socks-proxy-agent > socks > ip-address. pnpm audit exits 1 on any vulnerability finding by default, so the GHA job fails. This is not a real installation failure (deps install fine) but an audit/security gate failure — categorized as dependency_install since pnpm audit is part of the dependency-management toolchain. Source URL: https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/actions/runs/25434456587/job/74608664628. Imported on 2026-05-06; raw.log normalized from `rtk proxy gh run view --job <id> --log` output.
root_cause_summary: The `pnpm audit` step in pnpm/pnpm's Audit workflow reported 1 moderate-severity vulnerability — `ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods` (advisory GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g) — in the transitive dependency `ip-address` at vulnerable versions `<=10.1.0`, with patched range `>=10.1.1`. The dependency reaches the project through the path `network__fetch > @pnpm/network.agent > @pnpm/network.proxy-agent > socks-proxy-agent > socks > ip-address`. By default pnpm audit exits 1 on any vulnerability finding, which propagates to the GHA job's `##[error]Process completed with exit code 1.`. This is an audit/security gate failure, not an install failure — deps install fine; the fix is to bump the transitive `ip-address` to >=10.1.1.

notes: Renovate-style babel update broke 13 jest snapshots across 3 test suites in prettier's _errors_/import/format.test.js. babel changed how `import()` parser errors are reported (`Unexpected token (1:8)` -> `\`import()\` requires exactly one or two arguments. (1:1)`). multi_failure = true. signal_position = scattered (first FAIL block at L399 ≈17.5%, final summary at L2242 ≈98%). Source URL: https://github.com/prettier/prettier/actions/runs/25139423427/job/73685543084. Imported on 2026-05-06; raw.log normalized from `rtk proxy gh run view --job <id> --log` output.
root_cause_summary: Prettier's CI on the `renovate/babel` branch (a babel package update) broke 13 jest snapshots across 3 test suites because the updated babel parser reports `import()`-arity errors with new wording. The first failing test, `tests/format/js/_errors_/import/format.test.js > snippet: #0 > format[babel]`, raises `expect(received).rejects.toThrowErrorMatchingSnapshot()` and the snapshot diff shows `- Unexpected token (1:8)` (the previous babel error) replaced by `+ \`import()\` requires exactly one or two arguments. (1:1)` (the new babel error). Final totals: 3 failed test suites of 1503, 14 failed tests of 29653, 13 failed snapshots of 12099. The fix is either to re-run jest with `-u` to refresh the snapshots (if the new babel wording is accepted) or to pin babel back. The prettier source is correct — only the embedded babel diagnostic strings drifted.

notes: Spring Boot PR build (~16min). Gradle task `:module:spring-boot-actuator:checkFormatMain` failed because `src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/sbom/SbomProperties.java` has formatting violations. Late signal_position (failure summary at L6188-6195 of 6275, ~98%). Selected as Batch 5 case 1 — fills the v2 java-gradle ecosystem gap and is NOT in the calibration set used to tune hybrid-v2's 120k threshold. Source URL: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/actions/runs/25525927228/job/74921409232. Privacy audit: 0 hits, complete_scan=True. failure_category=lint_error (Gradle's checkFormat is a code-style lint rule); framework=generic because the runner is Gradle Build's Spring Boot meta-builder rather than tsc/jest/pytest/cargo enums.
root_cause_summary: Spring Boot PR build failed at the Gradle `:module:spring-boot-actuator:checkFormatMain` task because the file `src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/sbom/SbomProperties.java` has formatting violations under Spring Boot's Spring Java Format checker. The build's What-went-wrong block (L6190-6195) names the failed task and the single offending file and tells the developer to `Run \`format\` to fix.` This is a deterministic code-style gate; one task fails out of 2594 actionable Gradle tasks. The failure propagates: BUILD FAILED in 11m 27s (L6202) → ##[error]Process completed with exit code 1 (L6203).
